Tesla's Cybercab EPA certification documents reveal the robotaxi's full technical specs for the first time — including a 3,113-lb curb weight, a 219 HP motor, and a 48 kWh battery pack.Jun 15, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/06/15/tesla-cybercab-epa-specs-curb-weight-battery-motor-power/#:~:text=Tesla's%20Cybercab%20EPA%20certification%20documents,a%2048%20kWh%20battery%20pack.","title":"Tesla Cybercab full specs revealed: 3,113 lbs, 219 HP, 48 kWh | Electrek","content":"Tesla’s Cybercab EPA certification documents reveal the robotaxi’s full technical specs for the first time — including a 3,113-lb curb weight, a 219 HP motor, and a 48 kWh battery pack.
- Recharge energy (AC from wall): 53.365 kWh
At 3,113 lbs, the Cybercab is about 750 lbs lighter than a Tesla Model 3 Standard Range (~3,862 lbs) — but it has theww fewer seats, no steering wheel, no pedals, and a battery pack that’s roughly 12 kWh smaller.
At 47.6 kWh with a specific energy of 154 Wh/kg (as listed in the filing), the pack weighs roughly 680 lbs.
The EPA typically applies a correction factor of roughly 0.7 for electric vehicles to account for real-world conditions — air conditioning, heating, aggressive driving — which brings the adjusted range to approximately 293 miles.
Subscribe to Electrek on YouTube for exclusive videos and subscribe to the podcast."},{"url":"https://www.caranddriver.com/news/a71590701/tesla-cybercab-specs-epa-documents-revealed/","title":"New Tesla Cybercab Specs Have Been Revealed in EPA Documents","content":"With a roughly 50-kWh battery pack, the Cybercab could have just under 280 miles of EPA-estimated driving range.
To its credit, Tesla does operate a handful of Model Y Robotaxis without drivers in Texas.
Credit the publication of documents submitted by Tesla to the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), which include everything from curb weight to horsepower.
Additionally, the published information indicates that the Cybercab relies on a rather small battery pack with around 50 kWh of charge capacity."},{"url":"https://hypebeast.com/2026/6/tesla-cybercab-epa-specs-detail-weight-power-range","title":"Tesla Cybercab EPA Specs, Weight, Power and Range | Hypebeast","content":"Jun 18, 2026 — EPA documents reveal a 48-kWh battery pack and a 219-hp front-mounted … breaking efficiency rating of 165 Wh/mi, 163-kW front-mounted electric ...
Official EPA documents reveal a 48-kWh battery pack and a 219-hp front-mounted electric motor, achieving a laboratory-tested range of 418 miles
Recent regulatory filings with the Environmental Protection Agency provide the most comprehensive technical breakdown yet of the highly anticipated Tesla Cybercab.
Official documentation reveals a gross vehicle weight rating of 3,730 pounds.
Power for the localized transport pod comes from a single 163-kW front-mounted electric motor producing 219 horsepower.
New filings outline an auxiliary pack in a towable unit, managed alongside an 800V main battery for smarter fast charging."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/tech/tesla-cybercab-specs","title":"Tesla Cybercab specs reveal low weight, big range | Mashable","content":"According to Tesla's submission, the Cybercab will be powered by a single, 163kW (219HP) motor driving the front wheels, and a 47.6kWh lithium-ion battery.
While that battery capacity doesn't sound like much (for example, even the Rear-Wheel Drive Tesla Model 3 has a larger, 57.5kWh battery), the Cybercab is listed as having 418.2 miles of combined range, and 375.4 miles of highway range.
The impressive range is partially thanks to the Cybercab's low curb weight, which is officially 3,113 lbs (1,412kg).

Avatar for Fred Lambert  Fred Lambert | Jun 15 2026 - 9:38 am PT. Tesla’s Cybercab EPA certification documents reveal the robotaxi’s full technical specs for the first time — including a 3,113-lb curb weight, a 219 HP motor, and a 48 kWh battery pack. The Certificate Summary Information (CSI) for EPA test group TTSLV00.0L1A, filed on May 21 and certified on May 26, provides the most detailed look yet at the engineering behind the most efficient EV ever produced. * Curb weight: 3,113 lbs. At 3,113 lbs, the Cybercab is about 750 lbs lighter than a Tesla Model 3 Standard Range (~3,862 lbs) — but it has theww fewer seats, no steering wheel, no pedals, and a battery pack that’s roughly 12 kWh smaller. For a two-seat vehicle with no driver controls, 3,113 lbs is substantial.","score":0.92700076,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://hypebeast.com/2026/6/tesla-cybercab-epa-specs-detail-weight-power-range","title":"Tesla Cybercab EPA Specs Detail Weight, Power, Range","content":"EPA documents reveal a 48-kWh battery pack and a 219-hp front-mounted electric motor, rating of 165 Wh/mi, 163-kW front-mounted electric motor","score":0.90633047,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://electriccarsreport.com/2026/06/tesla-cybercab-epa-filing-reveals-official-range-power-and-battery-specs","title":"Tesla's Cybercab Gets Official EPA Range and Power Details","content":"Tesla Cybercab EPA certification documents reveal a 48 kWh battery, 219 hp front-wheel-drive motor, 3113-lb curb weight and estimated","score":0.8947989,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.tesery.com/blogs/news/tesla-cybercab-epa-specs-declassified-48-kwh-battery-418-mile-test-range-and-a-curb-weight-that-demands-explanation","title":"Tesla Cybercab EPA Specs 2026: 47.6 kWh Battery, 418- ...","content":"47.6 kWh battery, 418.2-mile MCT range, 163 kW motor, ... Motor: 163 kW (219 hp) Curb weight: 3,113 lbs (1,412 kg) — only ~750 lbs lighter","score":0.85825616,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://insideevs.com/news/798790/tesla-cybercab-specs","title":"Full Tesla Cybercab Specs: EPA Documents Confirm Battery Size, Weight, Power, And Preliminary Range","content":"# Full Tesla Cybercab Specs: EPA Documents Confirm Battery Size, Weight, Power, And Preliminary Range. ## The first front-wheel-drive Tesla will weigh way less than a Model 3 but still offer plenty of range. * The Tesla Cybercab will have 219 horsepower and a 48 kWh battery, new EPA filings confirm. * It'll weigh 3,113 lbs, making it the lightest EV on the U.S. market right now. * It looks like it'll have around 293 miles of range, based on preliminary testing figures adjusted by InsideEVs. The Tesla Cybercab just got one step closer to mass production. Thanks to the documents, which were first spotted by *Car and Driver* on Monday, we now know the official weight, power output, and battery size of the two-door robotaxi. Given that GVWR is the maximum combined weight of the car, its passengers, cargo, and fuel (not a factor here), we can do basic subtraction to learn that the Cybercab will have 617 lbs of payload capacity.","score":0.8364614,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.77,"request_id":"7f4c37b0-d4e4-41c8-8129-1cdf5873fcdf"}
